There's an important feature of exclusive information for you to know. When you get a new piece of information, for instance, an unpublished report comes across your desk, you hear a novel piece of information at a conference, or you see one emerge from marketing research, and it supports what you're trying to recommend. You should immediately get it out to those you want to influence. You could do that with a call, or an email, or text, or by getting in people's offices, where you should be sure to say that it's exclusive. No one else has this information. It's not even published yet. Or at least that it's fresh. I just got it this morning. Especially exclusive information is most powerful the newer it is. It's not like why. It doesn't get better with age, it's like bread. People want to take it in more when it's warm rather than cold, when it's fresh rather than stale.
